title: Child's Song.
content:
The King and the Queen were riding
    Upon a Summer's day,
    And a Blackbird flew above them,
    To hear what they did say.

    The King said he liked apples,
    The Queen said she liked pears.
    And what shall we do to the Blackbird
    Who listens unawares.
meaning:
Kate Greenaway's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Child's Song."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
